# Example 2B: Full 4-Layer Manifest

This is a documentation example showing the recommended 4-layer manifest
structure covering all nine semantic planning kinds.

Claim boundary: planning_artifact_only_not_pz_load_tested

---

## Manifests

Two manifest files are provided:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| layer-manifest.json | Documentation example (images not committed; hand-drawn) |
| generated-layer-manifest.json | Runnable example (images generated by new-example-images.ps1) |

Use generated-layer-manifest.json for end-to-end testing.

---

## Layer layout (both manifests)

| Layer | Image | Allowed kinds |
|---|---|---|
| terrain | terrain.png | grass, industrial_yard |
| roads | roads.png | road, sidewalk |
| buildings | buildings.png | row_house, depanneur, garage, landmark |
| markers | markers.png | spawn, landmark |

Precedence: markers > buildings > roads > terrain

---

## Deterministic layout (generated images)

### terrain.png (300x300)

- Background: grass (R:100 G:140 B:70)
- industrial_yard block at (210, 210) 80x80 -- bottom-right service zone

### roads.png (300x300)

- Background: grass
- Horizontal road at y=130, width=300, height=16 (rows 130..145)
- Vertical road at x=130, width=16, height=300 (cols 130..145)
- Sidewalk bands (4px each) above/below horizontal road and left/right of vertical road

### buildings.png (300x300)

- Background: grass
- row_house at (10, 10) 80x70 -- top-left residential cluster
- depanneur at (160, 160) 28x28 -- near intersection
- garage at (10, 200) 25x25 -- bottom-left service
- landmark at (260, 10) 20x20 -- top-right reference point

### markers.png (300x300)

- Background: grass
- spawn at (70, 70) 6x6 -- inside the row_house cluster (intentional overlap)

### Known conflict

The spawn marker at (70,70,6x6) overlaps the row_house block at (10,10,80x70).
Expected: 36 conflict cells. Markers wins (higher precedence): spawn chosen.
This is intentional -- it demonstrates that point markers override building footprints.

---

## How to generate images and run the pipeline

Step 1: Run the image generator.

    powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "tests\fixtures\layers\example-2b\new-example-images.ps1"

Images are written to tests/fixtures/layers/example-2b/generated/ (gitignored).

Step 2: Run layer-pipeline.

    dotnet run --project src/PZMapForge.Cli -- layer-pipeline ^
      --layers tests/fixtures/layers/example-2b/generated-layer-manifest.json ^
      --palette source/image-palette.json ^
      --output .local/mapforge

Expected output:
  Layers merged: 4
  Conflicts:     36
  Regions:       ~15
  Status:        OK

Step 3: Inspect the reports.

    .local/mapforge/layer-merge-report.md   -- conflict summary, per-layer stats
    .local/mapforge/plan-report.md          -- planning recommendations

---

## Why generated images are not committed

Generated PNG files are ignored by .gitignore (tests/fixtures/layers/example-2b/generated/).
The generator script (new-example-images.ps1) is committed instead.

This means the example is:
- Deterministic: same script always produces the same pixels
- Diff-friendly: changes to the layout are visible as script diffs, not binary diffs
- Correct: regenerating after a palette change keeps colors in sync

---

## Expected layer images (documentation example)

The four PNG images referenced by layer-manifest.json are not committed.
They should be 300x300 pixels painted with palette colours from
source/image-palette.json.

For hand-drawn images, follow the per-layer colour reference above.
For programmatic images, see new-example-images.ps1 and:
  tests/PZMapForge.Core.Tests/Layers/LayerMergerTests.cs (MakeSolid, MakeZone helpers)
  tests/PZMapForge.Cli.Tests/LayerPipelineProcessTests.cs (MakeGrassImage helper)
